title: InterPro  - Teaching & Learning - Tools & Technology - Canvas Assignments, Feedback, and Grading
body: Canvas Assignments, Feedback, and Gradebook Description Canvas offers several different ways to submit assignments and provide constructive feedback and enter student grades in the gradebook. This document provides resources for EPD instructors and students that will develop instructor presence in the online classroom and foster an interactive learning environment. Assignments Overview Assignments Overview Video (7:52) Assignment Submission (Instructors) Available Canvas assignment types Creating an online assignment Canvas - Creating Video Assignments Using Kaltura [UW-Madison] Adding or editing details to an assignment How to limit submission attempts How to bulk update due dates and availability InterPro  - Teaching & Learning - Tools & Technology - Turnitin Adding assignment groups Assignment Submission (Students) Assignment Submissions - video tutorial How to submit an assignment InterPro - Online Learning - Canvas - Creating and Submitting Digital Video Assignments Canvas - Submitting a Video Assignment Using Kaltura (Student) [UW-Madison] Submitting a media file Scanning submissions InterPro - Online Learning - Tools & Technology - Canvas - How to Post Audio Comments in Discussion Forum posts Assignment Feedback (Instructors) SpeedGrader What is SpeedGrader? SpeedGrader Overview - video tutorial How to Use SpeedGrader Leave Text, File, or Media (audio or video) comments on student submissions Using Chrome's speech recognition feature to leave a comment How to properly use audio feedback DocViewer Overview - video tutorial Adding annotated comments using DocViewer Using a rubric in SpeedGrader Using free-form comments in rubrics within SpeedGrader How do my students View SpeedGrader Annotations Bulk Download/Upload Bulk Download Assignment Submissions in Canvas Bulk Upload Assignment Submissions in Canvas Enter Grades Manually within the Gradebook Gradebook What is Gradebook? Gradebook Overview - video tutorial How to Use Gradebook Leave text comments for students Send a message to students who fit a specific criterion How to download all student submissions from the Gradebook How to upload all student submissions to the Gradebook Accessing SpeedGrader via the Gradebook Auto-grade missing assignments as zero in the grade book by applying a missing assignment policy Why it's important to enter missing assignments as zero in the Gradebook Canvas - Examining the Grade Posting Policy [UW-Madison] [Link for document 95543 is unavailable at this time] Rubrics What are rubrics? Quiz question types that offer automatic grading How to set up feedback for quiz questions (viewable after quiz submission) How to set up immediate feedback for students as they advance through a quiz, one question at a time Assignment Feedback (Students) Feedback Overview - video tutorial Canvas - Giving and Finding Feedback How to view assignment comments from an instructor Viewing quiz results and feedback InterPro - Online Learning - Tools and & Technology - Canvas - Peer Review an Assignment - for Students Limitations Canvas - Known Issue - Large Enrollment Gradebook Slowness [UW-Madison] See Also InterPro - Online Learning - Tools & Technology - Canvas - Canvas LMS Quick Start for InterPro Online Instructors InterPro - Online Learning - Tools & Technology - Canvas - LMS Quick Start for InterPro Online Students InterPro  - Teaching & Learning - Tools & Technology - Canvas Quizzing InterPro  - Teaching & Learning - Tools & Technology - Kaltura
